metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the AC2 baseline from '628 lines before' to the verified current count (617) and correct the proof narrative from '>=128 lines' to '>=117 lines' so the stated arithmetic matches the actual wc -l output.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'README is 628 lines before' as the baseline for its proof arithmetic, but the actual README.md is 617 lines (ends at line 617). This causes the narrative proof claim '>=128 lines of duplicated catalog were removed' to be arithmetically wrong: 617−500=117, not 128. The AC2 shell command itself (`[ "$(wc -l < README.md)" -le 500 ]`) is still mechanically correct, but the stated proof narrative misleads an executor about how much content must be removed.
findings.location: AC2 — 'README is 628 lines before; the `## CLI` catalog alone is ~165 lines'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'README is 628 lines before' to 'README is 617 lines before' and update 'proving >=128 lines of duplicated catalog were removed' to 'proving >=117 lines of duplicated catalog were removed'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit constraint (and corresponding AC shell check) that the bare-text `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` annotation must be preserved verbatim in README.md after trimming, since README is the PyPI long description and the MCP Registry verifies ownership against it.
findings.finding: The `## MCP server` section (README.md lines 525–542) contains the bare-text line `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` — an ownership annotation the MCP Registry verifies against the PyPI long description (README.md is the PyPI long description). The plan characterises the `## MCP server` section as a 'volatile catalog' to be trimmed and replaced with a link to `docs/mcp-reference.md`, but never acknowledges or protects this annotation. If the implementer removes or restructures the `## MCP server` section without preserving `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` verbatim in README.md, MCP Registry ownership verification will break silently — the annotation leaves no trace in any AC check.
findings.location: Scope section + README.md lines 525–542 (`## MCP server` / `mcp-name:` annotation)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: `grep -qF 'mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar' README.md || echo 'MISSING MCP REGISTRY ANNOTATION'` and add a note in Scope that the `mcp-name:` bare-text annotation must not be removed even when the surrounding `## MCP server` narrative is trimmed.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/manifest-signing.md` to the Files section and specify that if the `## Configuration` section anchor changes, that inbound link must be updated (or confirm the section is preserved with its anchor intact).
findings.finding: docs/manifest-signing.md:62 holds an inbound anchor link `[Configuration](../README.md#configuration)` targeting the `## Configuration` section of README.md. The plan's scope explicitly targets config catalogs for removal, and if the `## Configuration` section is removed or its anchor renamed, this link breaks — but docs/manifest-signing.md is not listed in the Files section and is not acknowledged as a consumer requiring update.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) keep `## Configuration` as a section heading (shrink its content but not the heading/anchor), OR (b) add `docs/manifest-signing.md` to the Files list and update its link to point at `docs/config.md` (already the authoritative config reference).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC or note that `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` must be retained verbatim in README.md after trimming the MCP section (it is a machine-read registry annotation that must remain in the PyPI long description).
findings.finding: The `## MCP server` section of README.md (lines 525–543) contains the bare text `mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar` which the MCP registry greps from the PyPI long description (README.md = the long-desc). The plan will trim this section and replace with a link to docs/mcp-reference.md but gives no instruction to preserve this annotation, which must remain somewhere in README.md regardless of how the section is restructured.
findings.location: Scope — 'trim the duplicated, volatile command/tool/config catalogs' / README.md lines 537–542
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line AC: `grep -qF 'mcp-name: io.github.navapbc/rebar' README.md || echo 'MISSING MCP ANNOTATION'` — this annotation must survive the trim and stay in README.md.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out statement (e.g. 'if generated references are found incomplete post-merge, restore via `git revert`; the catalog is preserved in git history') or reframe the `depends_on` sequencing as an explicit expand-contract step so the reversibility mechanism is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan performs a destructive, content-removing step (eliminating ~165+ lines of README catalog that current readers rely on) and acknowledges the risk ('removing content with no replacement'), but provides no explicit rollback or back-out mechanism. The expand-contract sequencing is only implicit (via the `depends_on` note) rather than stated as the reversibility strategy, and no fallback is described if the generated references prove incomplete after the catalog is deleted.
findings.location: What section ('Sequenced AFTER…') and AC2 ('README shrinks…catalog not removed')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short 'Rollback' or 'Reversibility' note in the plan body: 'This change is reversible via `git revert <merge-SHA>`; the catalog text is preserved in git history. The `depends_on` sequencing is the expand-contract gate — the generated references MUST be verified complete before this PR merges.'

findings.finding: AC2 has no inline automated proving command — it relies on a manual before/after `wc -l` comparison ('record in a comment') with no single command that fails if the threshold isn't met. An executor can satisfy this criterion by reporting numbers without the catalog actually being removed.
findings.suggested_fix: Store the pre-change line count in the plan/ticket as a literal (e.g. 'baseline: 312 lines') and add an inline command: `BEFORE=312; [ $(wc -l < README.md) -le $((BEFORE - 40)) ] || echo 'SHRINK THRESHOLD NOT MET'`

findings.finding: The Scope section requires preserving 'what/why' narrative and 'routing' sections, but AC3 only verifies `## Install` and `## Quickstart` headings — the 'what/why' and 'routing' sections have no corresponding verification, leaving those scope-required sections unchecked.
findings.location: Scope bullet 2 vs. Acceptance Criteria — AC3

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
